# Transcrição: 06. How to Gather Your Stories

- **Fonte:** 06. How to Gather Your Stories
- **Duração:** 01:49
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:49]

Now that you've invited your partner, let's talk about how to gather your stories. We're looking for standout memories, the experiences in your life that mattered most. These are the experiences and stories that have helped to shape who you are or that have had the most impact on you. These stories could be from as recently as yesterday and as far back as you can remember. You might have some that pop into your mind right away. If it takes a little more thought and recollection, that's okay too. Since these are things many of us rarely think about, we've included some optional prompts at the top of the story gathering page. Coming up next. Each prompt is designed to help you recall a specific experience or a person in your life that was particularly significant for you. You can skip the prompts if you want to, and decide to help rather than get in the way. Your goal is to gather a range of meaningful stories. Remember, the more impactful the stories are, and the more specific you can be about the people involved and the emotions you felt, the better. Think of times when you felt fulfilled and loved what you were doing. Times when you were doing something really meaningful that mattered to you. Challenging experiences may also come to mind, and these are just as important to capture if they had an impact on you. Think back to your early childhood, school years, and life inside and outside of work. Stories from all these areas of your life have the potential to be equally as important. When a story comes to mind, give it a title. Titles will help you recall the memories when you're with your partner in part two. These titles will be listed in the Partner Guide that you will share with your partner. Once your story has a title, type in the details in the description section.
